div.h1: Memory Layout: 4×4 Matrix Row-Major vs Column-Major

p.strong: Technical Specification for SIMD Data Alignment
p.em: Total Matrix Size: 64 Bytes (16 elements × 4 bytes)

text.tspan: Linear Address = Base + (row * width + col) * sizeof(T)
Linear Address = Base + (col * height + row) * sizeof(T)

div.h3: Matrix Multiply (C = A × B) Performance Analysis

p.em: For element $C_{ij} = \sum A_{ik} B_{kj}$, access pattern efficiency determines GFLOPS.

div.p: Architectural Optimization: Transposing the RHS matrix (B) to Column-Major format is a standard pre-processing step in BLAS libraries (Intel MKL/OpenBLAS) to ensure L1 Cache spatial locality.

ol.li: VADDPS ymm1, ymm1, [base+32] : Row 2 vector add

div.p: ✓ Contiguous Row Access (Spatial Locality) ✓ Direct DMA Mapping
✓ Contiguous Column Access ✗ Strided Row Access (L1 Cache Miss Risk in C loops)
